0.q: Because quinoa is an allotetraploid, we considered the possibility that homologs of REBC exist. BLAST analysis resulted in the identification of REBC-like1 and REBC1-like2.
REBC encodes a member of the family of WD‐40 domain‐containing proteins, which... in quinoa includes an additional member, REBC‐like1, the function of which is unknown.

3.q: CqSNAP30a enhances plant stress tolerance by regulation of ion homeostasis, adjustment of osmotic pressure, protection of the photosynthetic system, and enhancement of antioxidant capacity.

4.q: Biochemical study indicates that CqRALF15, a paralog of Arabidopsis RALF22, is physically associated with CrRLK1L proteins CqFER and AtFER.

5.q: CqEXPA50 interact with CqARF26, CqIAA2, CqGH3-14 and CqSAUR30 in N. benthamiana leaves. Green indicates a positive interaction signal. No signal was observed from negative controls.

6.q: CqEXPA50 interact with CqARF26, CqIAA2, CqGH3-14 and CqSAUR30 in N. benthamiana leaves. Green indicates a positive interaction signal. No signal was observed from negative controls.

7.q: CqEXPA50 interact with CqARF26, CqIAA2, CqGH3-14 and CqSAUR30 in N. benthamiana leaves. Green indicates a positive interaction signal. No signal was observed from negative controls.

8.q: CqEXPA50 interact with CqNHX4, CqCBL10 and CqHKT1 in N. benthamiana leaves. Green indicates a positive interaction signal. No signal was observed from negative controls.

9.q: CqEXPA50 interact with CqNHX4, CqCBL10 and CqHKT1 in N. benthamiana leaves. Green indicates a positive interaction signal. No signal was observed from negative controls.

10.q: CqEXPA50 interact with CqNHX4, CqCBL10 and CqHKT1 in N. benthamiana leaves. Green indicates a positive interaction signal. No signal was observed from negative controls.

11.q: BLAST analysis resulted in the identification of REBC-like1 and REBC1-like2. Relative to REBC protein, 46 C-terminal amino acid residues were deleted in REBC-like1, and 224 N-terminal amino acid residues were deleted in REBC-like2.

12.q: Three pairs of paralogous homologs (CqARF03 and CqARF15, CqARF05 and CqARF10, CqARF09 and CqARF17) were present for CqARF in Group1.

14.q: Combined expression profiling identified CqEXPA50 as being involved in auxin-mediated salt tolerance ... the auxin pathway and salt stress-related genes regulated by CqEXPA50 were identified.

15.q: The enhanced transcript levels of GH3.3 and AUX1 in CqKCS2B.1 OE#5 and OE#6 seedlings suggest that CqKCS2B.1 regulates lateral root formation by affecting auxin metabolism and transport.

16.q: the total amounts of aliphatic suberin monomers in CqKCS2B.1 OE#5 and OE#6 were increased by 14.48% and 15.49%, respectively, compared with that in WT.

17.q: CqKCS2B.1 plays an important role in the elongation of VLCFAs from C20 to C24.
